howItWorks:
Strip away the mystique, and ECDSA is just arithmetic on a curve — the very same arithmetic that locks every Bitcoin and Ethereum key on Earth. To a large enough quantum computer, that lock isn't a wall; it's a math problem with an answer. The question this benchmark asks is gloriously specific: how *cheap* can that computer be?

You submit a reversible quantum circuit that adds two points on the secp256k1 curve — the beating heart of Shor's algorithm — and your score is the bill it runs up: peak qubits × executed Toffoli gates. Lower is better. Build the leanest *correct* machine, and you've measured, down to the gate, exactly how exposed the world's keys really are.
whySound:
Here's the part that should keep a scientist up at night: a benchmark is only as honest as the exam it gives. The original ecdsa.fail picks its 9,024 test inputs by hashing your circuit's *own* instructions — which means the student is writing the exam.

Append a handful of do-nothing gates — free, they don't change the cost — and you quietly reshuffle which questions get asked, until you land on a set your circuit happens to pass. The circuit is still *wrong* on the inputs nobody looked at. We checked: a circuit sitting near the top of that board fails 17 to 22 of a fresh, random sample.

Google anticipated exactly this and proved their circuit correct with a zero-knowledge proof — a guarantee no amount of gate-shuffling can fake. So we forked it. Our grader draws its test inputs from a source the contestant cannot touch. Nonce-hunting does nothing. The only way to score is to build a circuit that is genuinely, verifiably correct — which, when you think about it, is the only number that was ever worth measuring.

That's why the gamed board's sub-1,300-qubit "circuits" vanish here, and why — on this board — honest is the hard mode.

reference.name: Google — low-qubit (ZKP-validated)
reference.note: Google's correct, ZKP-validated point-add (arXiv 2603.28846). 1,175 logical qubits — the target. Toffoli not directly comparable (their figure is for the full Shor run, not per point-add). They validated with a zero-knowledge proof precisely to make nonce-gaming impossible — the standard this fork enforces.

reference.name: Google — low-gate (ZKP-validated)
reference.note: Google's second operating point: 1,425 qubits, fewer Toffoli. Also ZKP-validated and correct.

entries.name: monolithic square + conditioned cmp
entries.note: Model: Claude Opus 4.8 — found by the AUTONOMOUS monitor loop (cron reseed -> daemon -> independent confirm), not a hand-launched panel. q x T SOTA: 6,493,016,160 -> 6,433,381,314 (-0.92%); peak HELD at 2034. STACKS two value-exact levers ON TOP of SOUND-OPT-5's phase-conditioned comparator: SQUARE_ROW_MAX_SEG=0 restores the bit-EXACT monolithic Solinas square (it removes the windowing-comparator overhead — the FULL-precision square, the opposite of a truncation), and the correction-wrap borrow trims round-84 bookkeeping. These touch different phases than the conditioned comparator, so they compose additively: avg-executed Toffoli 3,192,193 -> 3,162,920. Independently confirmed: peak 2034, 0 classical / 0 phase / 0 ancilla across 8 fresh OS-random seeds (re-check avg-T 3,163,028). Value-exact by construction (monolithic = the exact square) so there is no rare-input tail — unlike the rejected envelope-tightenings (CENSUS-iteration-floor-260613.md).

entries.name: phase-conditioned comparator
entries.note: Model: Claude Opus 4.8 — found by the 11-persona descent panel. The conditioned-replay base (held the q x T SOTA until SOUND-OPT-6, which stacks the monolithic square on top). First genuine ladder descent below 2034: q x T 6,911,739,468 -> 6,493,016,160 (-6.06%). Peak HELD at 2034 — the win is on the Toffoli axis. The apply-phase underflow comparator runs ~100-200x per point-add (every GCD apply-replay step) and is BOTH the sole 2034 peak-binder and the top Toffoli sink. Phase-conditioned replay measures its cleaning flag into a classical bit, then replays the predicate as a Toffoli ONLY on the ~50% of shots where the bit fired — halving that comparator's AVERAGE EXECUTED Toffoli (the grader charges per executed conditioned gate, so this is a real avg-executed-Toffoli cut, not a vanity emit-count). Toffoli 3,398,074 -> 3,192,240. Independently verified: peak 2034, 0 classical / 0 phase / 0 ancilla across 8 fresh OS-random seeds (even the worst-case seed beats the old SOTA). The verifier caught + worked around a concurrent session corrupting ops.bin mid-grade (sha256-guarded re-run in an isolated dir) — a non-skeptical check would have falsely failed a sound lever.

entries.name: borrowed-carry comparator
entries.note:
Model: Claude Opus 4.8 · the peak-2034 borrow base (held the q x T SOTA until SOUND-OPT-5).

Replaces the apply-phase underflow comparator with cmp_acc_plus_f_ge_p_measured_borrowed — the same predicate and measured Hmr-Gidney uncompute as the 2039 lever, but its four carry arrays BORROW their 256 clean |0⟩ lanes from the current compressed block instead of allocating new ones. That removes the peak-binding load_const(256).

Enable: DIALOG_GCD_UNDERFLOW_CLEAN_CMP=acc_plus_f_measured_borrowed (on top of the canonical sound config).

Independently verified on a fresh box: peak 2034, 0 classical / 0 phase / 0 ancilla across 8 fresh OS-random seeds, avg-T 3,398,102. Honest caveat: the borrowed-carry win saturates at the 5-cell compressed block — going lower needs a different structural lever (that's what the persona panel is hunting).

entries.name: measured SET-carry comparator
entries.note:
Model: Claude Opus 4.8.

Computes flag ^= (acc + f ≥ p) directly as the carry-out of acc + f + c (c = 2^256 − p, sparse) via a SET-carry majority recurrence (NOT XOR injection — that was the invalid 1926 over-claim), uncomputed with the measured sweep. This removes the 256-qubit load_const that bound the 2292 peak.

Verified: peak 2039, 0/0/0 across 8 fresh seeds, avg-T 3,398,114. A drop-in-equivalent predecessor to SOUND-OPT-3.

entries.name: windowed comparator carry
entries.note: Model: Claude Opus 4.8 — found by the 11-persona panel. LOWEST PEAK in the codebase (1926q, 1.64x Google): windows the apply comparator's four internal carry arrays so the peak collapses below the underflow-fold floor (binder moves to underflow_FOLD at 1926). Independently verified — peak 1926, 0 classical / 0 phase / 0 ancilla across 8 fresh OS-random seeds. HONEST TRADEOFF: q x T is the ranking metric, and the four boundary reconstructions cost +6% Toffoli (3.40M -> 3.60M), so the q x T SCORE regresses +0.43% vs the 2034 SOTA — a PEAK record, not a score win. The open problem is a Toffoli-cheap peak reduction (B=2 optimal; B=3 -> 1955).

entries.name: value-exact baseline
entries.note:
Model: Claude Opus 4.8.

The honest correct-circuit floor before the comparator levers: PROD65 + ROUND84_SQ_CARRY_HOST + a wide variable-width GCD envelope, with every truncation turned OFF. This is the genuinely-correct circuit the gamed board never had to build.

Verified: peak 2292, 0/0/0 across ~35 fresh seeds, avg-T 3,294,353.

entries.name: naive correct (full-width)
entries.note:
Model: Claude Opus 4.8.

Full-width, no value-exact levers — the most conservative correct circuit. Peak 2292, avg-T 3,847,387. Shown to quantify the value-exact levers: PROD65 + SQ_CARRY cut Toffoli ~14% (3.85M → 3.29M) at no peak cost.

invalid.name: race-1217
invalid.note:
INVALID — not a correct circuit.

The original-leaderboard "frontier" config (peak 1217). Its low Toffoli count comes from lazy-carry / windowed-comparator / width-envelope truncations that are WRONG on a fraction of reachable inputs. It validates only under ONE hunted DIALOG_TAIL_NONCE that steers the op-stream-seeded sample away from its errors.

Under this fork's grader (test inputs independent of the op stream): 17–22 classical + 10–13 phase mismatches on every fresh seed. It is shown here struck-through to make the point — this is what the gamed board ranks at the top.

invalid.name: active-iters-340 (our own sweep)
invalid.note: INVALID — iteration truncation, caught by OUR OWN convergence census (CENSUS-iteration-floor-260613.md). Cutting the binary-GCD inversion to 340 iterations drops the peak to 1879 (−155q, below even the 1926 record) and PASSES a K=8 fresh-seed sound-check — but the proven worst case is ≥372 iterations (witnesses p−262641→372, p−69→367, p−1→354), so the modular inverse is left UNFINISHED on a structured, reachable set of inputs. It passes K=8 only because that tail has measure ≈0 under uniform sampling (a 5M-sample census found max 272). Same pathology as race-1217 — shifted from nonce-steering to tail-evasion. The proven iteration floor is 402. Shown here on purpose: the board self-polices, and a K=8 pass is necessary, NOT sufficient.
